title: ArMythos
description: The Aristotelian dialect's primary record — mythos, 'the arrangement of the incidents', the soul of tragedy (Poetics 1450a), above character / thought / diction / melody / spectacle. Per design/aristotelian-sketch-01.md A1. Central events, a phase structure (A2), optional peripeteia / anagnorisis pointers (A4), the three unity assertions (A6), cross-dialect character records (A5), and an authorial catharsis claim (A8). The self-verifier's checks (A7) operate over the A3 complex-plot condition (enforced also by this schema's allOf clause), phase scope coverage, unity-of-time / unity-of-place bounds, event-ref integrity, and hamartia participation.

id.description: Stable identifier for this mythos (e.g., 'ar_oedipus'). PFS6-M2.

title.description: Title of the work under Aristotelian analysis (e.g., 'Oedipus Tyrannus'). PFS6-M2.

action_summary.description: Prose summary of the central action — what happens, not how it's narrated. The author's one-paragraph statement of the mythos. PFS6-M2.

central_event_ids.description: Substrate event ids comprising the central action. Order is preserved for author intent but not semantically required; the phase structure (A2) authorizes the arrangement. Non-empty: a mythos with zero events violates A1's 'arrangement of incidents' — there must be at least one incident. Plain-string references per PFS6-X2 / PFS5 OQ3. PFS6-M3.

plot_kind.description: Per A3: simple plots have a single change of fortune with no peripeteia / no anagnorisis; complex plots involve peripeteia, anagnorisis, or both. If 'complex', the allOf clause below requires at least one of peripeteia_event_id / anagnorisis_event_id. PFS6-M4.

phases.description: The three logical divisions of the mythos — beginning, middle, end — per A2. Minimum one phase; the self-verifier (A7 unity-of-action check) validates phase-coverage and disjointness against central_event_ids. Cross-file $ref to phase.json per PFS6-X1. PFS6-M5.

complication_event_id.description: Optional substrate event id marking the Aristotelian 'binding' (Poetics 1455b) — where beginning ends and middle begins. Plain-string reference per PFS6-X2. PFS6-M7.

denouement_event_id.description: Optional substrate event id marking the Aristotelian 'unbinding' (Poetics 1455b) — where middle ends and end begins. Plain-string reference per PFS6-X2. PFS6-M7.

peripeteia_event_id.description: Optional substrate event id where fortune reverses direction (Poetics 1452a). Interpretive pointer per A4 — the event structurally exists in the substrate; the 'reversal' claim is interpretive (substrate does not carry fortune-state per substrate-sketch-05's F1 retirement). Plain-string reference per PFS6-X2. PFS6-M7.

anagnorisis_event_id.description: Optional substrate event id where a character moves from ignorance to knowledge (Poetics 1452a). Per A4, anagnorisis is closer to structural than peripeteia — substrate's identity-and-realization machinery carries knowledge-state transitions, so an anagnorisis event IS a substrate-visible realization event. Plain-string reference per PFS6-X2. PFS6-M7.

asserts_unity_of_action.description: Aristotelian (Poetics 1451a) — defaults to true in Python. The A7 unity-of-action check runs unconditionally in the verifier; this field exists for authorial explicitness and for encodings that deliberately reject the assertion. PFS6-M8.

asserts_unity_of_time.description: Neoclassical-leaning (Aristotle mentions the 'single revolution of the sun' as a tendency, not a rule). Python default false. When true, the A7 unity-of-time check is applied against unity_of_time_bound. PFS6-M8.

asserts_unity_of_place.description: Strictly neoclassical (Castelvetro 1570) — not Aristotelian proper. Python default false. When true, the A7 unity-of-place check is applied against unity_of_place_max_locations. PFS6-M8.

unity_of_time_bound.description: Configurable bound for the unity-of-time check — max(τ_s) - min(τ_s) over central_event_ids must be ≤ this value when asserts_unity_of_time is true. Python default 24; unit is encoding-defined. Carried unconditionally by the Python dataclass; verifier reads it only when the assertion is true (PFS6's anticipated non-finding 2). PFS6-M9.

unity_of_place_max_locations.description: Configurable bound for the unity-of-place check — the distinct at_location set across central_event_ids must have cardinality ≤ this value when asserts_unity_of_place is true. Python default 1. Same unconditional-carry pattern as unity_of_time_bound. PFS6-M9.

aims_at_catharsis.description: Authorial claim about intended effect on pity and fear (A8). The self-verifier does NOT check catharsis — audience response is out of scope for the substrate + dialect stack. This field records the claim. Python default true. PFS6-M10.

characters.description: Optional ArCharacter records authored at mythos scope per A5. Empty array is legal (a mythos without Aristotelian-dialect characters is valid — characters may live on the Dramatic side only, or be elided for short-form probes). Cross-file $ref to character.json per PFS6-X1. Python default is an empty tuple. PFS6-M11.

anagnorisis_chain.description: Optional staggered character-level recognitions supplementing the singular anagnorisis_event_id, per aristotelian-sketch-02 A11. Empty array is legal and is the Python default; pre-sketch-02 encodings carry no chain. A7.7 enforces the A11 invariants (step event_ids in central_event_ids; steps distinct from the main anagnorisis; precipitates_main → τ_s precedes the main; character_ref_id resolves in characters). Cross-file $ref to anagnorisis_step.json per PFS13-X. PFS13-M12.

peripeteia_anagnorisis_binding.description: Optional structural claim about the relation between peripeteia_event_id and anagnorisis_event_id, per aristotelian-sketch-02 A12. Three canonical values: 'coincident' (equal event ids), 'adjacent' (τ_s distance ≤ peripeteia_anagnorisis_adjacency_bound), 'separated' (τ_s distance > bound). None / absent means no structural claim (pre-sketch-02 default). A7.8 enforces substrate-consistency of the declared value when substrate events are threaded. Closed enum at schema level; unknown values emit the `peripeteia_anagnorisis_binding_invalid_value` observation from the verifier as a second line of defense for Python-level misvalues that bypass the schema. PFS13-M13.

peripeteia_anagnorisis_adjacency_bound.description: Configurable τ_s bound for the peripeteia_anagnorisis_binding='adjacent' check — |τ_s(peripeteia) − τ_s(anagnorisis)| ≤ this value. Python default is 3 (Oedipus-scale pacing); encodings with a different dramatic clock (decades-long fabula; single-dramatic-day compression) override. Carried unconditionally by the dataclass; verifier reads it only when peripeteia_anagnorisis_binding='adjacent' (same unconditional-carry pattern as unity_of_time_bound and unity_of_place_max_locations). Per PFS13-M14 / OQ4, no schema-level default is declared — Python carries it. PFS13-M14.

allOf.description: Complex-plot conditional per A3 + A7 check 1 + PFS6-M6: if plot_kind is 'complex', at least one of peripeteia_event_id or anagnorisis_event_id must be present. Extends PFS5-B5's if-then-else pattern with an anyOf-in-then shape.
